mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 20d returns in > 55% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient supply-side capacity that absorbs rate hikes without triggering immediate earnings downgrades, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles are followed by negative 5d returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and persistent high inflation creates a stagflationary risk premium that compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and CPIAUCSL exceeds 190.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly expand household income and aggregate consumption, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes against 20d returns remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm an economic contraction that forces corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60d returns in > 55% of confirmed recession quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal accelerating economic momentum that boosts short-term market sentiment, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5d returns with a success rate above 52% in walk-forward testing.
